volterraSturmLagrangeIdentityStatus.label: Volterra/Sturm Lagrange identity
volterraSturmLagrangeIdentityStatus.reason: Green's formula D_a B_P[K,f]=K Lambda_a(f)-f P^*K is verified symbolically for the moving trace operator.

distributionalInteriorGreenSourceStatus.label: distributional interior Green source
distributionalInteriorGreenSourceStatus.reason: The exact jump matrix represents each local source row as the singular part of P^*K at s0.

exactEndpointFredholmReductionStatus.label: exact endpoint Fredholm reduction
exactEndpointFredholmReductionStatus.reason: After the jump is fixed, the exact fundamental matrix gives P_delta Beta_z=M z+b(d), solvable iff b(d) lies in Range(M).

formalGreenAnnihilationImplicationStatus.label: formal Green annihilation implication
formalGreenAnnihilationImplicationStatus.reason: If the endpoint Fredholm compatibility is satisfied for an active source row, the resulting Green coefficient gives ell(f)=int_I K(a)Lambda_a(f) da, so closed trace implies ell(f)=0.

finiteOffBaseDerivativeRankUCStatus.label: finite off-base derivative-rank UC
finiteOffBaseDerivativeRankUCStatus.reason: Full rank of the off-base derivative response matrix proves the finite active unique-continuation implication on Galerkin active blocks.

sourceSideResponseNoncollapseStatus.label: source-side response noncollapse
sourceSideResponseNoncollapseStatus.reason: The Riesz-projector rank certificate proves noncollapse of the selected off-base derivative response on the certified source active plane.

endpointCompatibilityForActiveRowsStatus.label: endpoint compatibility for active source rows
endpointCompatibilityForActiveRowsStatus.reason: Closed either by rigorous full active row rank of M, which makes ker(M^*) trivial, or by combining the Green pairing formula with the Volterra/Sturm primal obstruction annihilation theorem.

closedTraceActiveUniqueContinuationStatu.label: closed-trace active unique continuation
closedTraceActiveUniqueContinuationStatu.reason: Closed if the exact Green annihilation implication is available for every scalar active source row.  The remaining open part is endpoint/Fredholm compatibility in the continuum exact Green BVP.

derivativeRankEvidence.interpretation: Stable full derivative rank at off-base points is the finite Galerkin shadow of active unique continuation.  The continuum proof must show that these confluent derivative matrices converge on the spectral active spaces in the A-graph norm.

projectiveResponseEvidence.interpretation: Direct projective convergence should make these normalized response columns Cauchy after sign/permutation alignment.  Large distances mean the current Galerkin active eigenlines are not yet a stable sequence in the chosen response coordinates.

sourceSideNoncollapseEvidence.theorem: For S=E^*E, ||S-S_N||<=eps and eps<gap/4 imply ||P-P_N||<=4eps/gap for the active top-two Riesz projectors. Since sigma_min(LP_N)=m and ||L||=ell, every unit v in Range(P) satisfies ||Lv||>=m(1-alpha)-ell alpha.  The displayed positive lower bound proves rank(L on Range(P))=2, equivalently rank(LE^*|U_delta)=2 for the positive source eigenspace.

endpointFredholmObstructionFormulaStatus.label: endpoint Fredholm obstruction formula
endpointFredholmObstructionFormulaStatus.reason: The exact endpoint problem is Mz=-b(d); compatibility is w^*b(d)=0 for every w in ker M^*.

greenPairingFormulaForObstructionStatus.label: Green pairing formula for obstruction
greenPairingFormulaForObstructionStatus.reason: Green's identity and the exact jump law give w^*b(d)=int K_d P f_w - d(f_w) for the primal endpoint test f_w.

finiteSampledObstructionAnnihilationStat.label: finite sampled near-obstruction annihilation
finiteSampledObstructionAnnihilationStat.reason: The resolved relative-threshold left endpoint direction must annihilate the actual source endpoint vectors.  This is stronger than solving the finite endpoint equation with the full sampled matrix.

volterraSturmPrimalObstructionAnnihilati.label: Volterra/Sturm primal obstruction annihilation
volterraSturmPrimalObstructionAnnihilati.reason: Still need the non-circular continuum theorem: every primal homogeneous endpoint obstruction f_w annihilates the actual active source rows d_u.

continuumEndpointObstructionCompatibilit.label: continuum endpoint obstruction compatibility
continuumEndpointObstructionCompatibilit.reason: Closed either by rigorous full active row rank of M, which makes ker(M^*) trivial, or by combining the Green pairing formula with the Volterra/Sturm primal obstruction annihilation theorem.

endpointObstructionCompatibilityTheorem.remainingAnalyticGap: Build a stable validated endpoint-flow solver that encloses the exact fundamental matrix Phi(a,s0) and endpoint rows with Frobenius endpoint-map radius below the rank margin.

proof: Let P f=Lambda_a(f).  The exact Lagrange identity gives d B_P[K,f]/da=K P f-f P^*K.
For a source row ell, choose K piecewise homogeneous away from s0 with the exact jump Delta(ell), so P^*K=ell as a distribution.
Integrating Green's formula over I yields ell(f)=int_I K(a)Lambda_a(f) da plus endpoint concomitants.
The exact endpoint fundamental-matrix theorem reduces those endpoint concomitants to Mz+b(ell).  If b(ell) lies in Range(M), choose z=-M^+b(ell) and the active endpoint term vanishes.
Therefore, for every closed-trace f with Lambda_a(f)=0 on I, the row ell(f) is zero.  Applying this to the active source basis gives E_active f=0.

The off-base derivative-rank and source-side noncollapse certificates support the same conclusion from the analytic trace-response side, but the exact Green proof still needs the continuum endpoint compatibility for the actual source rows.
remainingAnalyticGap: Build a stable validated endpoint-flow solver that encloses the exact fundamental matrix Phi(a,s0) and endpoint rows with Frobenius endpoint-map radius below the rank margin.
